meaning: adj.迅速的,飞快的,险峻的n.急流,高速交通工具,高速交通网
examples: The disease is spreading at a rapid rate.
The area has seen a rapid population growth.
It seems reasonable to expect rapid urban growth.
Labour leader Ed Miliband called for a "rapid response" from the government to help affected communities.
The raft smacks into the first rapid and baptizes us with an icy blast.
